creator: val@packett.cool
text:
Hm, looks like the problem just disappeared after I… added a new fan to the system and went into the UEFI Setup to configure fan speed??

Maybe there was something wrong with ACPI or something and saving the firmware settings fixed them… Interesting that it didn't affect Windows 10 though.

(In reply to Eric van Gyzen from comment #2)
Desktop Ryzen is not NUMA, it only has one domain. Threadripper has two domains, EPYC has four, but Ryzen only one. It has some 'NUMA-like' cache groups as recognized in 'FreeBSD/SMP: 1 package(s) x 2 cache groups x 4 core(s) x 2 hardware threads', but this is not actual NUMA.
